title: Pseudolepanthes colombiae
taxonomy: Pseudolepanthes colombiae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Pseudolepanthes, which is a part of the subtribe Cranichidinae. This plant is native to Colombia and its classification is closely related to other orchids found in the same region. Further classification includes it being placed within the tribe Cranichideae and family Orchidaceae. Its specific classification provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationship with other orchids.

The health benefits of Pseudolepanthes colombiae are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress.
